Did you know chlorine doesn’t just stay in the water—it clings to your skin and floats in the air you breathe? 😷

When chlorine mixes with things like sweat, sunscreen, hair, or lotion, it forms byproducts (including compounds like haloacetic acids) that can contribute to skin and eye irritation and may irritate the airways—especially for little ones who spend lots of time in the pool.

Every time we got out of the pool, my daughter would itch nonstop and her skin was red and irritated.

That’s why I created Chlorine Cleanse—a vitamin C–based spray that neutralizes chlorine on your skin after swimming.

✨ Safe for babies, toddlers & sensitive skin
✨ Helps reduce that lingering chlorine residue, dryness & “pool smell”
✨ Non-toxic, fragrance-free, and made in small batches
✨ Great after swimming and after using public pools or showers

💛 Because every bottle is handmade in small batches, I only open the shop for limited windows throughout the summer.

The shop closes July 17, and once this batch sells out, it’ll be unavailable until the next restock.

If you’ve been thinking about trying it, this is your reminder not to wait. ☀️

Comment SPRAY and I’ll DM you the link. 💌

items.caption:
We were never meant to do this alone.

Somewhere along the way, we lost the village - but we never lowered the expectations placed on mothers.

We’re still expected to be everything to everyone: the caregiver, the teacher, the chauffeur, the chef, the planner, the comforter, the employee, the partner, the friend... all while smiling through exhaustion and making it look effortless.

Today, many mothers are doing it with little support, limited time, and the constant pressure to keep up with impossible standards.

We weren’t designed to carry every responsibility alone or to feel guilty for needing help. 

Maybe it’s time to stop asking mothers to do more and start asking how we can support them better. 

#momlife #toddlermom #momsofinstagram #motherhood #newmom

This was one of the coolest parts of labor for me- especially watching it back and realizing how instinctual everything was. 

In the middle of labor, I suddenly had this overwhelming urge to get down on my hands and knees. No one told me to do it- my body just knew. 

As soon as I was on my hands and knees, I realized how uncomfortable the bathroom floor was so I stood up, walked to the birthing tub and proceeded to start pushing. 

Shortly after, I was holding my baby in my arms. 

If you let it, birth has a way of letting your body just take over. 

If you’re preparing for birth and want to feel more informed and empowered, I put together a free birth guide with things I wish I knew before labor, postpartum prep, mindset tips, and more. 

Comment BIRTH & I’ll send it to you 🖤 (be sure your following or you won’t see the message!) 

#positivebirth #birthpreparation #birthplan #unmedicatedbirth #homebirth

You probably already baby-proofed your outlets, cabinets, and stairs…

items.caption:
I accidentally created a product over one parenting rabbit hole. 😂

When my daughter was little, every single time we’d get out of the pool, her skin would be so dry, itchy, and irritated. I assumed that was just part of swimming… until I started researching.

That’s when I learned that simply rinsing off isn’t always enough to get rid of chlorine. So I started making a homemade vitamin C spray to use after every swim.

It worked so well that friends started asking me to make it for them too.

The only problem? Homemade versions don’t stay fresh for very long, and I got tired of mixing up a new batch every couple of weeks.

So I spent months creating a shelf-stable version that I could actually keep in my pool bag all summer.

That’s how Chlorine Cleanse was born. 💛

✨ Vitamin C–based spray that helps neutralize chlorine after swimming
✨ Gentle for babies, toddlers, and sensitive skin
✨ Fragrance-free and made in small batches
✨ Helps with that dry, tight, “pool skin” feeling (and yes… the chlorine smell too 🙌)

I only open the shop for limited windows throughout the summer because every bottle is handmade in small batches.

This batch closes July 17, and once it’s gone, it’ll be unavailable until the next restock.

If you’ve been meaning to try it, don’t wait. ☀️

Comment SPRAY and I’ll send you the link. 💌

#chlorine #momhack #momlife #crunchymama #firsttimemom

items.caption:
I get asked about this swim vest every single summer, so I figured it deserved its own post. ☀️💦

When Adalynn was 3, we tried private swim lessons… and it was a complete disaster. She went from LOVING the water to being afraid to get in.

Instead of forcing it, we focused on making the pool fun again. This swim vest gave her the confidence to explore, move naturally in the water, and build her skills at her own pace.

One of my favorite features is that you can remove the floats as your child becomes more comfortable, so it grows with them instead of becoming useless after one season.

Just to be clear—I’m not saying to skip swim lessons. Every child is different, and this was simply what worked best for our family.

If you’re looking for an alternative to puddle jumpers this summer, this is the one I’d choose every time. 💙

Comment “SWIM” and I’ll send you the link! 🏊

#toddler #toddlerswimming #learningtoswim #toddlermom #momlife @limmys.official
items.transcript: My four-year-old taught herself how to swim completely on her own after using this vest. It keeps them in the proper forward position and allows them to fully use their arms. Plus, it has removable floats to increase the difficulty.

items.caption:
I remember this moment so clearly… I was so out of it—laughing, swaying, barely forming sentences—and yet my body knew exactly what to do.

No epidural, no meds… just my body flooding me with its own natural “drugs” (hello endorphins + oxytocin 🫶) to get me through one of the most intense things I’ve ever done.

It’s wild to look back at this and realize:
✨ I wasn’t losing control… I was surrendering to it
✨ That “loopy” feeling? My body protecting me
✨ We are SO much more capable than we’re led to believe

If you’re pregnant or planning an unmedicated birth—this is your reminder that your body was designed for this 🤍 

Follow along for more pregnancy, birth, postpartum + motherhood! 🖤

#unmedicatedbirth #birthstory #positivebirth #laboranddelivery #momlife
items.transcript: You feel the baby? Put your hand down and feel?

items.caption:
We made a quick stop at Trader Joe’s on the way home today and grabbed everything to make sandwiches instead of hitting the drive-thru. 🥪

Ever since I started learning more about what’s actually in a lot of fast food, I just can’t bring myself to feed it to my kids anymore. 🙈

That said… I also don’t know if I’m quite the mom who has her life together enough to never rely on fast food. 😂

So I’m curious…

What are your go-to options when you’re out with your kids and need something quick? Are there any healthier drive-thru meals, grocery store grab-and-go meals, or restaurants you genuinely feel good about?

Because I’m definitely looking for better “we’re on the go” options. 👇

#relatablemom #crunchymom #fastfoodsucks #toddlermom #motherhoodreality
items.transcript: We just stopped at Trader Joe's to grab food because I could not fathom to bring myself to pay for any of the fast food that we passed. I just like know what's in it and I couldn't. And now I don't know. I don't know how I feel about that.

items.caption:
Like truly, half the time I spend trying to work as hard as possible to make more money because I know our bills need to get paid, and it would be nice to be able to afford a van…. But then when I am working, I’m wishing that I was spending every second with my kids because this is IT. 

This is my only opportunity to live their childhoods, work will always be there.. right?

I shared this exact video a year ago when we first got the Tiny Traveler monitor. We've now been using it for a long time, so I'm going to share my honest opinion. Overall, the functionality is way better than a mirror. You can actually see your child, see what they're doing, and see them in the dark. It comes with a carrying case and it's super convenient to travel with. We actually use this a lot, especially when we're just like at grandma's house. However, here are the few problems we have noticed. One is as my kids get older, they try to kick it and knock it off. This has resulted in several broken cords that we've had to replace. Another thing that we've had to replace is the actual mount that holds the monitor. It just would not stick on our car, so we got our... Neither of these were deal breakers. We still absolutely love it and I highly recommend.

items.transcript: These baby proofing products saved my sanity. As you can see, kids do not realize that big pieces of furniture can fall over and smash them. Personally, I will not take the chance. I love these damage-free anchors. They're really easy to install. Some furniture like this vanity, also the mirror in our bedroom, is pretty tricky, so I love these adhesive ones that attach to anything. I cannot even tell you how many times this has already come in handy. We have been using these no-show cabinet locks for the past three years on basically all of the cabinets in our house. We actually use this cord hider not only aesthetically, but as a safety precaution because both my kids have tried to rip down their baby monitors using the cord and even chewed on it. We've got a lot of stairs in our home, and even a crawling baby can move faster than you think. Gates are a must. And last but not least are these adhesive locks. We use them on so many different things, closets, drawers, garbage, everything.

items.transcript: TocaToca

items.caption:
I don’t.

No countdown. No “last feed” circled on the calendar. 

Just following their lead.

Letting my little one self-wean isn’t always easy. 

But watching them still find comfort and nourishment even past the age of 1 is so rewarding.

Allowing them to gently let go in their own time reminds me to trust them and their timing.

Breastfeeding is never just about milk. 

It’s about sleepy cuddles, quiet comfort, and a bond I’ll carry forever. 

To every mama navigating the bittersweet journey of self-weaning: it’s okay to celebrate, to grieve, and to cherish it all at the same time. ❤️

Did you let your baby self-wean? How long did your breastfeeding journey last? Tell me in the comments!
items.transcript: Hello.

items.caption:
Every birth is different (and just as incredible) but these are specific things that were said to me while I was pregnant with my first and wanted to have an unmedicated birth. 😳

1. “Labor is excruciating!” 
It doesn’t have to be. 
I’ve had 2 unmedicated births now and “excruciating” isn’t how I would describe either of them. 
Intense? Yes. Powerful? Absolutely 

2. “Birth plans are pointless”
I disagree. 
Having a plan is incredibly important- even if it doesn’t go perfectly! It opened conversations with my provider, clarified my values, and helped my support team advocate for me when I was busy 😉

3. “You’ll ask for drugs in the moment- I promise”
The amount of people who told me that my goals were unrealistic was wild. 
Unmedicated birth is VERY possible. 
If this is something you want, you CAN do it. 
But preparation matters- education, mindset, and support. 

4. “You need to decide how you want to labor” 
NOPE. 
I chose a provider and a setting that gave me freedom.
Standing, laying, rocking, walking, or in the tub.
I was able to fully surrender and allow my body to decide in the moment. 

5. “An epidural is the only way to enjoy a birth”
Nope :)
My daughter’s birth felt euphoric- like my body was doing exactly what it was designed to do. 

My son’s home birth was grounding, powerful, and one of the most empowering experiences of my life. 

👉 Whether you choose medicated, unmedicated, hospital, or home- you deserve support, not fear. 

If you’re preparing for birth and want encouragement (no horror stories), follow along and be sure to check my bio for my free birth guide. 🖤

#positivebirth #pregnant #unmedicatedbirth #birthstory #newmom

items.caption:
A few years ago, I shared a simple homemade Vitamin C spray that I made to help neutralize chlorine on my daughter’s skin and hair after swimming.

I posted the recipe on my blog and social media, and thousands of moms started making it for their own families.

The feedback was amazing… except for one thing.

The homemade version only stayed fresh for about a day.

As much as moms loved it, most of us don’t have the time to make a fresh batch every time we’re headed to the pool, swim lessons, or a water park.

So I started researching.

After a lot of testing, I created a shelf-stable formula using a gentle, eco-certified preservative that keeps it fresh for up to 2 months.

That’s how Chlorine Cleanse was born.

What started as a homemade solution for my daughter has now helped thousands of families across the country make pool season a little easier. 💙

If you’d like to learn more about Chlorine Cleanse and how it works, comment SPRAY below and I’ll send you the details + the link! 💦 

#summer #summertime #crunchymom #swimmingpooltime #toddler
